title: Compound Interpretive Value Dossier
description: A single dossier carrying a compound interpretive question decomposed into ordered, individually-interpretive sub-claims over a shared subjects map. Each sub-claim is adjudicated under a declared weighting lens; the judge composes the sub-verdicts. Evidence is deliberately rich and multi-typed: the classification of an evidence type into a tier, and its relevance to a given lens, is itself contestable — that ambiguity is the framework's attack surface.

$framework.description: Framework pin metadata (id/ipfs/slug/version). Echoed for provenance; not load-bearing for adjudication.

asOf.description: Snapshot date the dossier was assembled as-of. The judge treats this as the recency anchor.

question_frame.description: Declares the resolution frame. compound_interpretive is the only frame v1.0 supports.

compoundQuestion.description: The full natural-language compound question, e.g. 'Is Bellingham Real Madrid's most valuable player in 2023-24 AND is his value primarily output-driven AND is he stronger in big games than season-average?'

compositionRule.description: How sub-verdicts compose into the on-chain outcome. v1.0 supports AND only.

primarySubjectClaimId.description: Which claim's primarySubject becomes the on-chain subject_ref. MUST reference a claim whose primarySubject is a primary-tier subject.

claims.description: Ordered list of individually-interpretive sub-claims. Each is adjudicated under its own lens, then composed.

claim.description: Natural-language sub-claim. MUST be genuinely interpretive (contested weighting), not an objective fact lookup.

claimType.description: The interpretive operation the sub-claim performs.

interpretiveLens.description: The weighting frame applied to the shared evidence for THIS claim. The same evidence weighs differently under different lenses.

primarySubject.description: Dossier key of the subject this claim is about.

evidenceMapping.description: Explicit pointers from this claim to the dossier evidence paths it is permitted to rely on, grouped by tier. The judge MUST NOT leverage Tier-1 evidence for a claim that this mapping omits, even if the field is loud in the prose.

crossClaimRefs.description: Declared coherence constraints the judge must validate across sub-claims. A violation makes the compound UNRESOLVABLE.

expectedConsistency.description: e.g. 'implies_driver_consistency' (if c1 resolves on irreplaceability evidence, c2 cannot claim output is the primary driver).

tier_assignments.description: Top-level declaration of which subjects are direct comparands (primary) vs framing (context).

context_notes.description: Top-level long-form framing. Load-bearing prose; read it, but it is Tier-3 by default.

subjects.description: Shared subjects map. All claims reference these same subjects under different lenses.

metricBlock.description: A measured-evidence block. snapshots[] carries temporal history; latest asOf is canonical. Inner content is permissive so attacks can shape it freely.

on_off_splits.description: TIER 1. Team output with player on vs off the pitch.

team_share.description: TIER 1. Player's % share of team xG/progression/touches.

big_game_splits.description: TIER 1 but small-sample. Per-90 in finals/derbies/knockouts vs season average. Load-bearing for the big_game_vs_average lens; treat as Tier-2 when sample is thin.

availability_record.description: TIER 1. Minutes played, injury/absence rate. A multiplier on value-to-club.

progression_metrics.description: TIER 1, AMBIGUOUS DRIVER. Carries, line-breaking passes, final-third entries. Reads as 'output' under one lens and 'irreplaceability' under another — classification is contested.

peer_valuations.description: TIER 2. Market valuations and actual bids.

salary_share.description: TIER 2. Salary as % of wage bill.

percentile_ranks.description: TIER 2, LENS-AMBIGUOUS. Percentile vs positional peers — citable as output (output lens) OR as value (default lens).

tactical_role_profile.description: TIER 2, AUTHORITATIVE-SOUNDING. Analyst characterisation of role centrality. Tempting to leverage as irreplaceability evidence though it is interpretation, not measurement.

manager_quotes.description: TIER 3. Conflict of interest. outcome_claims never load-bearing.
